records.ety: [L. supererogatus, p. p. of supererogare to spend or pay out over and above; super over, above + erogare to expend or pay out money from the public treasury after asking the consent of the people. See Super-, and Erogate, Rogation.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Supererogated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Supererogating.]

defs: To do more than duty requires; to perform works of supererogation; to atone (for a dificiency in another) by means of a surplus action or quality.
quotes: The fervency of one man in prayer can not supererogate for the coldness of another.
